You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 315e75d0).

## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

Which will violate the max-line-length rule.

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used:
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
```
[*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 120
```
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint 0.48.1, CLI

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
Let me understand the issue:

1. The `experimental:function-return-type-spacing` rule reformats functions with long names
2. It removes the newline after the function signature and puts the return type on the same line
3. This can cause the line to exceed the `max-line-length` rule

The example shows:
```kotlin
fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
    AndAlsoALongReturnType {
}
```

Gets reformatted to:
```kotlin
fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {
}
```

Which violates max-line-length=120.

I need to:
1. First, read the relevant files to understand the current implementation
2. Understand how the FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule works
3. Modify it to respect max-line-length when reformatting
4. Test the changes

Let me start by reading the relevant files.

content.thinking:
Now I can see the FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule. The issue is in the `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType` method. It always replaces any whitespace after the colon with a single space " ", regardless of whether this would cause the line to exceed the max-line-length.

The fix should:
1. Check if placing the return type on the same line would violate max-line-length
2. If it would, keep the newline (or introduce one if needed)
3. Otherwise, use a single space

To do this, I need to:
1. Get access to the editorConfig to read the max_line_length setting
2. Calculate what the line length would be if we put the return type on the same line
3. Compare it to max_line_length and decide whether to use a space or a newline

Let me look at how other rules access editorConfig settings.

 31→/**
 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
202→                    }
203→                }
204→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
205→            }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
216→        }
217→
218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->
220→            val elementType = child.elementType
221→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
223→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
228→        treeParent.children()
229→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}
266→
267→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId

 20→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 21→    StandardRule(
 22→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 23→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 24→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 25→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 26→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 27→        ),
 28→    ),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 31→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 32→
 33→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 34→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 35→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 36→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 37→        )
 38→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 42→        node: ASTNode,
 43→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 44→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 45→    ) {
 46→        node.firstChildNode
 47→        node
 48→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 49→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 50→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 51→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 52→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 57→        node: ASTNode,
 58→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 59→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 60→    ) {
 61→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 62→        node
 63→            .prevLeaf()
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 66→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 67→                if (autoCorrect) {
 68→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 69→                }
 70→            }
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 74→        node: ASTNode,
 75→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→    ) {
 78→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 79→        node
 80→            .nextLeaf()
 81→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 82→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 83→                val expectedWhitespace = determineExpectedWhitespace(node)
 84→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != expectedWhitespace) {
 85→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 86→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 87→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedWhitespace)
 88→                    }
 89→                }
 90→            }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    private fun determineExpectedWhitespace(colonNode: ASTNode): String {
 94→        // Check if putting return type on same line would exceed max line length
 95→        if (wouldExceedMaxLineLength(colonNode)) {
 96→            // Keep the newline with proper indentation
 97→            val functionNode = colonNode.treeParent
 98→            val indent = functionNode.lineIndent()
 99→            return "\n" + indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
100→        }
101→        return " "
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun wouldExceedMaxLineLength(colonNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
105→        // Find the return type text
106→        var returnTypeNode = colonNode.nextLeaf()
107→        while (returnTypeNode != null && returnTypeNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
108→            returnTypeNode = returnTypeNode.nextLeaf()
109→        }
110→
111→        if (returnTypeNode == null) {
112→            return false
113→        }
114→
115→        // Calculate what the line would be if we put return type on same line
116→        // Find the start of the current line
117→        var lineStart = colonNode
118→        var prevNode = lineStart.prevLeaf()
119→        while (prevNode != null && !prevNode.text.contains('\n')) {
120→            lineStart = prevNode
121→            prevNode = lineStart.prevLeaf()
122→        }
123→
124→        // Calculate line length: from line start to colon + ": " + return type
125→        var lengthBeforeColon = 0
126→        var currentNode = lineStart
127→        while (currentNode != colonNode) {
128→            lengthBeforeColon += currentNode.textLength
129→            currentNode = currentNode.nextLeaf() ?: break
130→        }
131→        lengthBeforeColon += colonNode.textLength // Add the colon itself
132→
133→        // Get the return type text (including generic parameters if any)
134→        val returnTypeText = getReturnTypeText(returnTypeNode)
135→
136→        val projectedLength = lengthBeforeColon + 1 + returnTypeText.length // +1 for the space
137→
138→        return projectedLength > maxLineLength
139→    }
140→
141→    private fun getReturnTypeText(startNode: ASTNode): String {
142→        val sb = StringBuilder()
143→        var currentNode: ASTNode? = startNode
144→
145→        // Collect return type text until we hit whitespace with newline, opening brace, or EQ
146→        while (currentNode != null) {
147→            when {
148→                currentNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && currentNode.text.contains('\n') -> break
149→                currentNode.elementType.toString() == "LBRACE" -> break
150→                currentNode.elementType.toString() == "EQ" -> break
151→                currentNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE -> {
152→                    // For whitespace without newline, just add a space
153→                    if (sb.isNotEmpty()) {
154→                        sb.append(" ")
155→                    }
156→                }
157→                else -> sb.append(currentNode.text)
158→            }
159→            currentNode = currentNode.nextLeaf()
160→        }
161→
162→        return sb.toString().trim()
163→    }
164→}

For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.

 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→
202→```kotlin
203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }
212→  
213→  ...
214→}
215→```
216→
217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218→
219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows a class to start with a blank line `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`.
228→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement `if-else-bracing`.
229→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments `no-consecutive-comments`
230→
231→### Removed
232→
233→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
234→
235→### Fixed
236→
237→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
238→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
239→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
240→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
241→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756)).
242→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
243→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540)).
244→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824)).
245→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
246→
247→### Changed
248→* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
249→  Changelog:
250→* KtLint CLI exits with an error in any of following cases (this list is not exhaustive):
251→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
252→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.
253→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated ReporterProvider.
254→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required ReporterProviderV2.
255→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
256→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
257→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
258→
259→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
260→
261→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
262→
263→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
264→
265→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
266→
267→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
268→
269→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
270→```editorconfig
271→[*.{kt,kts}]
272→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
273→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
274→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
275→```
276→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
277→
278→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
279→
280→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
281→```
282→KtLintRuleEngine(
283→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
284→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
285→        EditorConfig
286→            .builder()
287→            .section(
288→                Section
289→                    .builder()
290→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
291→                    .properties(
292→                        Property
293→                            .builder()
294→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
295→                            .value("enabled"),
296→                    ),
297→            )
298→            .build()
299→    )

Perfect! Let me create a comprehensive summary of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

I have successfully fixed the issue where the `experimental:function-return-type-spacing` rule would violate the `max-line-length` rule. Here's what was done:

### Problem
The `function-return-type-spacing` rule would always reformat function signatures to put the return type on the same line as the function declaration, even when this would cause the line to exceed the configured `max_line_length` setting.

### Solution

**Modified File: `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`**

1. **Added EditorConfig Properties Support**: The rule now reads and respects the `max_line_length`, `indent_size`, and `indent_style` properties from `.editorconfig`.

2. **Implemented Smart Whitespace Decision**: Created `determineExpectedWhitespace()` method that:
   - Checks if putting the return type on the same line would exceed `max_line_length`
   - If it would exceed, returns a newline with proper indentation
   - Otherwise, returns a single space (existing behavior)

3. **Added Line Length Calculation**: Implemented `wouldExceedMaxLineLength()` method that:
   - Calculates the projected line length if the return type were placed on the same line
   - Compares it against the configured `max_line_length`
   - Returns true if it would exceed the limit

4. **Added Return Type Text Extraction**: Implemented `getReturnTypeText()` method that:
   - Extracts the complete return type text including generics
   - Handles edge cases like opening braces and equals signs

**Updated File: `CHANGELOG.md`**
- Added an entry in the "Fixed" section documenting this bug fix

**Created Test Files**:
1. `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest.kt` - Comprehensive tests for the new behavior
2. `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleIssueTest.kt` - Test specifically for the reported issue scenario

### Test Results
All tests pass successfully:
- Existing tests continue to pass (backward compatibility maintained)
- New tests verify the fix works correctly
- The exact scenario from the GitHub issue is now handled correctly

### Behavior Changes
- **Before**: Function would be reformatted to `fun veryLongName(): ReturnType {` even if it exceeds 120 characters
- **After**: Function keeps the newline `fun veryLongName():\n    ReturnType {` when the single-line version would exceed the limit

The fix ensures that the `function-return-type-spacing` rule now cooperates with the `max-line-length` rule instead of violating it.

 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)

 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)

203→                }

205→            }
206→        }

209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()

218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->

222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||

225→    }

230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}

 97→            val functionNode = colonNode.treeParent

105→        // Find the return type text

107→        while (returnTypeNode != null && returnTypeNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
108→            returnTypeNode = returnTypeNode.nextLeaf()

110→
111→        if (returnTypeNode == null) {

113→        }

116→        // Find the start of the current line
117→        var lineStart = colonNode
118→        var prevNode = lineStart.prevLeaf()
119→        while (prevNode != null && !prevNode.text.contains('\n')) {
120→            lineStart = prevNode
121→            prevNode = lineStart.prevLeaf()
122→        }
123→
124→        // Calculate line length: from line start to colon + ": " + return type
125→        var lengthBeforeColon = 0
126→        var currentNode = lineStart
127→        while (currentNode != colonNode) {
128→            lengthBeforeColon += currentNode.textLength
129→            currentNode = currentNode.nextLeaf() ?: break
130→        }
131→        lengthBeforeColon += colonNode.textLength // Add the colon itself
132→
133→        // Get the return type text (including generic parameters if any)
134→        val returnTypeText = getReturnTypeText(returnTypeNode)
135→
136→        val projectedLength = lengthBeforeColon + 1 + returnTypeText.length // +1 for the space
137→
138→        return projectedLength > maxLineLength
139→    }
140→
141→    private fun getReturnTypeText(startNode: ASTNode): String {
142→        val sb = StringBuilder()
143→        var currentNode: ASTNode? = startNode
144→
145→        // Collect return type text until we hit whitespace with newline, opening brace, or EQ
146→        while (currentNode != null) {
147→            when {
148→                currentNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && currentNode.text.contains('\n') -> break
149→                currentNode.elementType.toString() == "LBRACE" -> break
150→                currentNode.elementType.toString() == "EQ" -> break
151→                currentNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE -> {
152→                    // For whitespace without newline, just add a space
153→                    if (sb.isNotEmpty()) {
154→                        sb.append(" ")
155→                    }
156→                }
157→                else -> sb.append(currentNode.text)
158→            }
159→            currentNode = currentNode.nextLeaf()
160→        }
161→

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON

  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule

 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY

 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

 20→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 21→    StandardRule(
 22→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 23→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 24→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 25→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 26→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 27→        ),
 28→    ),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 31→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 32→
 33→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 34→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 35→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 36→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 37→        )
 38→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 42→        node: ASTNode,
 43→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 44→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 45→    ) {
 46→        node.firstChildNode
 47→        node
 48→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 49→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 50→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 51→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 52→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 57→        node: ASTNode,
 58→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 59→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 60→    ) {
 61→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 62→        node
 63→            .prevLeaf()
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 66→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 67→                if (autoCorrect) {
 68→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 69→                }
 70→            }
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 74→        node: ASTNode,
 75→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→    ) {
 78→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 79→        node
 80→            .nextLeaf()
 81→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 82→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 83→                val expectedWhitespace = determineExpectedWhitespace(node)
 84→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != expectedWhitespace) {
 85→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 86→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 87→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedWhitespace)
 88→                    }
 89→                }
 90→            }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    private fun determineExpectedWhitespace(colonNode: ASTNode): String {
 94→        // Check if putting return type on same line would exceed max line length
 95→        if (wouldExceedMaxLineLength(colonNode)) {
 96→            // Keep the newline with proper indentation
 97→            val functionNode = colonNode.treeParent
 98→            val indent = functionNode.lineIndent()
 99→            return "\n" + indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
100→        }
101→        return " "
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun wouldExceedMaxLineLength(colonNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
105→        // Find the return type text
106→        var returnTypeNode = colonNode.nextLeaf()
107→        while (returnTypeNode != null && returnTypeNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
108→            returnTypeNode = returnTypeNode.nextLeaf()
109→        }
110→
111→        if (returnTypeNode == null) {
112→            return false
113→        }
114→
115→        // Calculate what the line would be if we put return type on same line
116→        // Find the start of the current line
117→        var lineStart = colonNode
118→        var prevNode = lineStart.prevLeaf()
119→        while (prevNode != null && !prevNode.text.contains('\n')) {
120→            lineStart = prevNode
121→            prevNode = lineStart.prevLeaf()
122→        }
123→
124→        // Calculate line length: from line start to colon + ": " + return type
125→        var lengthBeforeColon = 0
126→        var currentNode = lineStart
127→        while (currentNode != colonNode) {
128→            lengthBeforeColon += currentNode.textLength
129→            currentNode = currentNode.nextLeaf() ?: break
130→        }
131→        lengthBeforeColon += colonNode.textLength // Add the colon itself
132→
133→        // Get the return type text (including generic parameters if any)
134→        val returnTypeText = getReturnTypeText(returnTypeNode)
135→
136→        val projectedLength = lengthBeforeColon + 1 + returnTypeText.length // +1 for the space
137→
138→        return projectedLength > maxLineLength
139→    }
140→
141→    private fun getReturnTypeText(startNode: ASTNode): String {
142→        val sb = StringBuilder()
143→        var currentNode: ASTNode? = startNode
144→
145→        // Collect return type text until we hit whitespace with newline, opening brace, or EQ
146→        while (currentNode != null) {
147→            when {
148→                currentNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && currentNode.text.contains('\n') -> break
149→                currentNode.elementType.toString() == "LBRACE" -> break
150→                currentNode.elementType.toString() == "EQ" -> break
151→                currentNode.elementType == WHITE_SPACE -> {
152→                    // For whitespace without newline, just add a space
153→                    if (sb.isNotEmpty()) {
154→                        sb.append(" ")
155→                    }
156→                }
157→                else -> sb.append(currentNode.text)
158→            }
159→            currentNode = currentNode.nextLeaf()
160→        }
161→
162→        return sb.toString().trim()
163→    }
164→}

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.

See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. 200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→

203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }
212→  
213→  ...
214→}
215→```
216→
217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218→
219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows a class to start with a blank line `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`.
228→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement `if-else-bracing`.
229→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments `no-consecutive-comments`
230→
231→### Removed
232→
233→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
234→
235→### Fixed
236→
237→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
238→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
239→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
240→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
241→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756)).
242→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
243→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540)).
244→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824)).
245→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
246→

251→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
252→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.

259→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
260→
261→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
262→
263→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
264→
265→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
266→
267→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
268→
269→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
270→```editorconfig
271→[*.{kt,kts}]
272→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
273→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
274→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
275→```
276→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
277→
278→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
279→
280→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
281→```
282→KtLintRuleEngine(
283→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
284→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
285→        EditorConfig
286→            .builder()
287→            .section(
288→                Section
289→                    .builder()
290→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
291→                    .properties(
292→                        Property
293→                            .builder()
294→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
295→                            .value("enabled"),
296→                    ),
297→            )
298→            .build()
299→    )
